problem_statement: Vibe coding / unstructured agent-driven development of AI-generated code suffers from architectural drift, limited traceability, and reduced maintainability/controllability, creating a conflict between development acceleration and end-product quality that needs structural oversight.
magnitude_evidence: In the Shift-Up run, 68 user stories and 175 executable acceptance test cases were generated; 176 prompts were categorized across the structured vibe coding and Shift-Up approaches. Prior study (Pattyn & Goetz) reports vibe coding's promises come 'at the cost of quality of prototypes, maintainability and rework iterations'.
insufficiency_evidence: In structured vibe coding, more than half of all prompts (52%) were dedicated to addressing issues identified manually in GUI or IDE; developer role reduced to reactive debugging and manual verification; RQ2 on drift reduction could only be partially answered because the small common-domain project prevented observing drift.
solution_mechanisms: executable requirements as guardrails (BDD/Robot Framework acceptance tests)
implementation roadmap divided into dependency-ordered phases
GitHub issues with test-pass close constraints
self-validating agent loop: run tests, feed failures back until pass
shift-up: humans focus on high-level design and acceptance in V-model
